channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Eddie Murphy’s Early Years on Saturday Night Live and His Lasting Impact
item.description: Eddie Murphy joined Saturday Night Live at just 19 years old, during a period when the show was struggling after the departure of its original cast and creator, Lorne Michaels, in 1980. His arrival re...

item.title: Tanzania Yet to Follow EAC Court Ruling on Kenyan Match Imports
item.description: Tanzania imekabiliwa na lawama baada ya kudaiwa kushindwa kutekeleza amri iliyotolewa na Mahakama ya Umoja wa Afrika Mashariki (EACJ) kuzuia kodi ya ziada ya sh 400 kwa kilo kwenye mpira wa usalama un...

item.title: TMRC and Habitat for Humanity Introduce Microfinance Housing Loans for Low-Income Tanzanians
item.description: The Tanzania Mortgage Refinance Company (TMRC), working with Habitat for Humanity International, just launched new housing microfinance products to help poor families, especially those working in info...

item.title: Government to Make Civil Servants’ Asset Declarations Public in Redacted Format to Ensure Transparency
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The government has announced that asset declarations of civil servants, as required under IMF governance and anti-corruption benchmarks, will now be made publicly accessible in a redacted f...

item.title: Understanding Group Dynamics and Workplace Pressure in Team Decisions
item.description: A workplace contributor shared their experience of colleagues turning against them during a meeting, attributing it to the influence of a powerful and vindictive personality. Associate Professor Conni...

item.title: Labour’s Achievements and the Need for Bold Leadership Change
item.description: Polly Toynbee argues that while the Labour Party under Keir Starmer has implemented significant reforms, such as improvements to child poverty, education, the NHS, workers’ rights, and green energy in...

item.title: Vigilante Anti-Immigration Marches in South Africa Raise Legal and Safety Concerns
item.description: In recent months, South Africa has seen a surge in marches organised by groups campaigning against so-called 'illegal immigration' and crime, with events drawing large crowds in cities such as Durban,...

item.title: Comelec Prepares for BARMM Parliamentary and Barangay Elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has confirmed that preparations are nearly complete for the upcoming elections in the Philippines. Comelec Chairman George Erwin Garcia stated that the Bangsamoro...

item.title: Springbok Prop Asenathi Ntlabakanye Suspended for 18 Months Following Doping Tribunal Ruling
item.description: Springbok prop Asenathi Ntlabakanye has been handed an 18-month ban after admitting to the use of an illegal substance. The ruling, effective from 13 May 2026, follows an adverse test showing traces o...

item.title: Reflections on Being Mistaken for Stephen Lewis
item.description: Steven Lewis, a Canadian health policy expert, recounts his unusual experience of sharing a name and identity confusion with the late Stephen Lewis, a renowned politician and international statesman. ...

item.title: Dr. Gikonyo Explains Medical Records in Court Regarding Gachagua Case
item.description: In Nairobi, Cardiologist Dr. Daniel Kibuka Gikonyo appeared in court to defend the accuracy of his medical records related to former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua. Lawyers had questioned variation...

item.title: NSW Blues secure 2026 Women’s State of Origin series with strong defence
item.description: NSW Blues have claimed back-to-back Women’s State of Origin titles after a thrilling 14-10 win over the Queensland Maroons at Suncorp Stadium. The match was tightly contested, with both teams exchangi...

item.title: Student passes away amid meningitis outbreak in Oxfordshire
item.description: A tragic incident has occurred in Oxfordshire where a student has died following a meningitis outbreak. The local health authorities have confirmed that several students were affected, and emergency m...

item.title: Ramaphosa removes Tolashe as Social Development Minister amid accountability concerns
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has dismissed Sisisi Tolashe from her position as Minister of Social Development, a move that has garnered widespread political commentary. The African National Congress (ANC...

item.title: President Zardari awards military honours to martyred and serving officers
item.description: On Thursday, President Asif Ali Zardari presided over a distinguished investiture ceremony at Aiwan-e-Sadr, conferring 47 military awards on officers and soldiers of Pakistan’s armed forces. The cerem...

item.title: Election Commission launches third phase of Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls across 16 states and 3 UTs
item.description: The Election Commission of India has announced the third phase of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls, covering 16 states and three Union Territories. This brings the voter list re...

item.title: Vice Ganda Collaborates with “It’s Showtime” Writers for Improvised Comedy
item.description: ABS-CBN continues to innovate in Philippine television as comedian Vice Ganda takes on a unique approach to comedy by working directly with the writers of the popular variety show “It’s Showtime.” The...

item.title: Francesca Annis: A Retrospective of Her Iconic Career and 1960s–70s Portraits
item.description: Francesca Annis stands as a timeless figure in British theatre and cinema, celebrated for a career spanning more than six decades. She first captured international attention as Lady Macbeth in Roman P...

item.title: Labour Leadership Uncertainty Intensifies Following Streeting Resignation and Burnham Speculation
item.description: The future of Labour leadership has become increasingly uncertain after Wes Streeting resigned as health secretary, criticising a lack of vision and direction under Sir Keir Starmer. Speculation has g...

item.title: Australia names full-time Counter-Terrorism Coordinator following anti-Semitism Royal Commission
item.description: The Australian government has appointed Brendan Dowling as a full-time Counter-Terrorism Coordinator within the Home Affairs Department, responding to recommendations from the anti-Semitism Royal Comm...
